(("Polycystic Ovary Syndrome"[Mesh] OR PCOS[tiab] OR polycystic ovary syndrome[tiab]) AND ("neck circumference"[tiab] OR "neck girth"[tiab] OR "neck size"[tiab] OR "collar size"[tiab]) AND (metabolic[tiab] OR "Metabolic Syndrome"[Mesh] OR "Insulin Resistance"[Mesh] OR insulin[tiab] OR glucose[tiab] OR "Blood Pressure"[Mesh] OR triglyceride*[tiab] OR lipid*[tiab] OR "Glucose Metabolism Disorders"[Mesh]))

inclusion_criteria: Observational human studies (cross-sectional, case-control, or cohort) including women diagnosed with PCOS using recognized clinical criteria (e.g., Rotterdam, NIH, AE-PCOS) or as defined in the primary study.
Include a comparison group: non-PCOS healthy women and/or intra-PCOS groups defined by neck circumference (e.g., larger vs smaller NC).

Report at least one outcome of interest: neck circumference; metabolic syndrome prevalence; insulin resistance indices (HOMA-IR, HOMA%S, fasting insulin); anthropometrics (waist, hip circumference); blood pressure; lipid values (e.g., triglycerides); or glucose metabolism parameters.
Provide extractable comparative data (e.g., means/proportions with variability or sufficient statistics) between specified groups.
exclusion_criteria: Non-observational designs (interventional trials), case reports/series, reviews, editorials, or conference abstracts without sufficient extractable data.
Studies without an appropriate comparator (neither non-PCOS controls nor NC-based subgroups) or not reporting any prespecified outcomes.
PCOS diagnosed solely by self-report or administrative codes without clinical criteria; mixed-sex or mixed-diagnosis samples without separable data for women with PCOS.
screening_criteria.description: Screen studies of women with PCOS that compare against non-PCOS controls or against PCOS subgroups by neck circumference and report neck circumference and/or metabolic, anthropometric, lipid, blood pressure, or glucose metabolism outcomes using observational designs with extractable data.

Adolescent PCOS and metabolic health: An analysis of fat, muscle, and hormones.
Higher Visceral and Lower Peripheral Adiposity Characterize Fat Distribution and Insulin Resistance in Asian Indian Women with Polycystic Ovary Syndrome in Mauritius.
Adolescent "Lean PCOS" Is Characterized by Higher Insulin Resistance and Adverse Adipokine Profile.
Polycystic Ovary Syndrome and Metabolic Syndrome: Clinical and Laboratory Findings and Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease Assessed by Elastography.
Insulin Resistance, Hyperinsulinemia, and Mitochondria Dysfunction in Nonobese Girls With Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome.
